front: O Carnaval de rua atrai milhões de foliões fantasiados todos os anos.
pronunciation: oh kahr-nah-VAHL jee RROO-ah ah-TRAHEE mee-LYOHNGZ jee foh-lee-OHNGZ fahn-tah-zee-AH-doohz TOH-doohz ohz AH-noohz.
back: Street Carnival attracts millions of costumed revelers every year.
context: Describing the massive informal street parties ('blocos') that dominate Brazil during Carnival.

front: O desfile das escolas de samba no Sambódromo é um espetáculo visual incrível.
pronunciation: oh dez-FEE-lee dahz es-KOH-lahz jee SAM-bah noo sam-BOH-droh-moo eh oong ees-peh-TAH-koo-loo vee-zoo-AHL een-KREE-vehl.
back: The samba schools' parade in the Sambadrome is an incredible visual spectacle.
context: Highlighting the organized, competitive parades of Rio de Janeiro and São Paulo.

front: Em Salvador, a multidão segue o trio elétrico pulando ao som do axé.
pronunciation: ehng sahl-vah-DOHR, ah mool-tee-DOHNG SEH-gee oh TREE-oo eh-LEH-tree-koh poo-LAHN-doh AH-oo sohm doh ah-SHEH.
back: In Salvador, the crowd follows the sound truck, jumping to the sound of axé music.
context: Describing the famous Bahian Carnival format using mobile sound stages called 'trios elétricos'.

front: O frevo é uma dança típica de Pernambuco que exige muita agilidade.
pronunciation: oh FREH-voo eh OO-mah DAN-sah TEE-pee-kah jee pehr-nahm-BOO-koo keh eh-ZEE-zhe MWEE-tah ah-zhee-lee-DAH-jee.
back: Frevo is a typical dance from Pernambuco that requires a lot of agility.
context: Introducing the fast-paced, umbrella-spinning dance of Recife and Olinda's Carnival.

front: As Festas Juninas celebram santos populares com muita dança de quadrilha.
pronunciation: ahz FES-tahz zhoo-NEE-nahz seh-LEH-brahm SAN-toohz poh-poo-LAH-reez kohm MWEE-tah DAN-sah jee kwah-DREEL-yah.
back: June Festivals celebrate popular saints with a lot of square dancing.
context: Explaining 'Festa Junina', Brazil's second-largest celebration honoring St. John, St. Peter, and St. Anthony.

front: Durante o mês de junho, comemos canjica, pamonha e bolo de milho.
pronunciation: doo-RAHN-tee oh MEHS jee ZHOONG-yoo, koh-MEH-mohz kahn-ZHEE-kah, pah-MOHNG-yah ee BOH-loo jee MEEL-yoo.
back: During the month of June, we eat hominy, pamonha, and corn cake.
context: Listing classic corn-based traditional foods of the winter June harvests.

front: Acender a fogueira de São João é um rito tradicional de gratidão.
pronunciation: ah-sehn-DEHR ah foh-GAY-rah jee SOHNG zhoo-ONG eh oong REE-too trah-dee-see-oh-NAHL jee grah-tee-DOHNG.
back: Lighting the Saint John bonfire is a traditional ritual of gratitude.
context: Highlighting the symbolic significance of bonfires in rural and urban June celebrations.

front: O Saci-Pererê é um garoto de uma perna só que adora fazer travessuras.
pronunciation: oh sah-SEE peh-reh-REH eh oong gah-ROH-too jee OO-mah PEHR-nah SOH keh ah-DOH-rah fah-ZEHR trah-veh-SOO-rahz.
back: Saci-Pererê is a one-legged boy who loves to play pranks.
context: Introducing Brazil's most famous folklore character, known for wearing a red cap and smoking a pipe.

front: O Curupira tem os pés virados para trás para enganar os caçadores.
pronunciation: oh koo-roo-PEE-rah teheng ohz PEHZ vee-RAH-doohz PAH-rah TRAHZ PAH-rah eeng-gah-NAHR ohz kah-sah-DOH-rez.
back: Curupira has backward-facing feet to trick hunters.
context: Describing the mythical red-haired guardian of the forests and protector of animals.

front: Diz a lenda que a Iara atrai os homens para o fundo do rio com seu canto.
pronunciation: DEEZ ah LEHN-dah keh ah ee-AH-rah ah-TRAHEE ohz OH-mehengz PAH-rah oh FOON-doo doh REE-oo kohm SEH-oo KAHN-toh.
back: Legend has it that Iara lures men to the bottom of the river with her singing.
context: Explaining the indigenous Amazonian mermaid/water nymph myth.

front: A lenda do Boto cor-de-rosa diz que ele se transforma em um belo homem.
pronunciation: ah LEHN-dah doh BOH-too kohr-jee-ROH-zah DEEZ keh EH-lee see trans-FOHR-mah ehng oong BEH-loo OH-meheng.
back: The legend of the pink river dolphin says he transforms into a handsome man.
context: Explaining the famous Amazonian myth of a shape-shifting dolphin seducing women at river dances.

front: O Bumba meu boi mistura teatro, dança e música dramática no Maranhão.
pronunciation: oh BOOM-bah MEH-oo BOY mees-TOO-rah teh-AH-troo, DAN-sah ee MOO-zee-kah drah-MAH-tee-kah noo mah-rahng-YOHNG.
back: Bumba meu boi mixes theater, dance, and dramatic music in Maranhão.
context: Highlighting a major cultural festival depicting the death and resurrection of a beloved ox.

front: O Festival de Parintins apresenta a disputa entre os bois Caprichoso e Garantido.
pronunciation: oh fes-tee-VAHL jee pah-reeng-TEENS ah-preh-ZEHN-tah ah dees-POO-tah EHN-tree ohz BOHEEZ kah-pree-SHOH-zoo ee gah-rahng-TEE-doh.
back: The Parintins Festival features the dispute between the oxen Caprichoso and Garantido.
context: Describing the massive theatrical folklore competition held annually in Amazonas state.

front: O Círio de Nazaré em Belém reúne milhões de fiéis em uma das maiores procissões.
pronunciation: oh SEE-ryoo jee nah-zah-REH ehng beh-LEHNG rrehoo-OO-nee mee-LYOHNGZ jee fee-EH-eez ehng OO-mah dahz mah-YOH-reez proh-see-SOHNGZ.
back: The Círio de Nazaré in Belém gathers millions of devotees in one of the largest processions.
context: Highlighting the monumental religious and cultural event of northern Brazil.

front: A Folia de Reis é uma festa católica que celebra a visita dos Reis Magos.
pronunciation: ah foh-LEE-ah jee REH-eez eh OO-mah FES-tah kah-TOH-lee-kah keh seh-LEH-brah ah vee-ZEE-tah dohz REH-eez MAH-goohz.
back: Folia de Reis is a Catholic festival celebrating the visit of the Three Wise Men.
context: Explaining the folkloric, musical parade of travelers going door-to-door between Christmas and Epiphany.

front: A tradicional lavagem das escadarias do Bonfim une sincretismo e fé.
pronunciation: ah trah-dee-see-oh-NAHL lah-VAH-zhehm dahz ees-kah-dah-REE-ahz doh bohng-FEENG OO-nee seeng-kreh-TEEZ-moo ee FEH.
back: The traditional washing of the Bonfim steps unites syncretism and faith.
context: Highlighting Bahia's historic interfaith event combining Catholicism and Afro-Brazilian Candomblé.

front: O Maracatu é um ritmo forte com herança das cortes reais africanas.
pronunciation: oh mah-rah-kah-TOO eh oong REET-moo FOHR-tee kohm eh-RAN-sah dahz KOHR-teez rreh-AHEEZ ah-free-KAH-nahz.
back: Maracatu is a strong rhythm inherited from African royal courts.
context: Describing the thunderous drum procession of Pernambuco, depicting coronation ceremonies of Congolese kings.

front: A Festa do Divino é marcada pela distribuição de doces e bandeiras coloridas.
pronunciation: ah FES-tah doh jee-VEE-noo eh mahr-KAH-dah PEH-lah dees-tree-boo-ee-ZOHNG jee DOH-seez ee bahn-DAY-rahz koh-loh-REE-dahz.
back: The Feast of the Divine is marked by the distribution of sweets and colorful flags.
context: Describing an old Portuguese-rooted community festival celebrated in historical towns like Paraty.

front: A literatura de cordel conta histórias folclóricas rimadas impressas em folhetos.
pronunciation: ah lee-teh-rah-TOO-rah jee kohr-DEHL KOHN-tah ees-TOH-ryahz fohl-KLOH-ree-kahz rree-MAH-dahz eem-PREHS-sahz ehng fohl-YEH-toohz.
back: Cordel literature tells rhyming folkloric stories printed in pamphlets.
context: Introducing northeastern Brazil's popular illustrated folk literature, hung on string lines ('cordel').

front: A roda de capoeira combina elementos de arte marcial, esporte e música.
pronunciation: ah ROH-dah jee kah-poh-AY-rah kohm-BEE-nah eh-leh-MEHN-toohz jee AHR-tee mahr-see-AHL, ees-POHR-tee ee MOO-zee-kah.
back: The capoeira circle combines elements of martial art, sport, and music.
context: Defining the cultural practice of Capoeira, recognized as an intangible cultural heritage.

front: Os pescadores oferecem flores e perfumes para Iemanjá no dia dois de fevereiro.
pronunciation: ohz pes-kah-DOH-rez oh-feh-REH-sehm FLOH-reez ee pehr-FOO-meez PAH-rah ee-eh-mahn-ZHAH noo DEE-ah doyz jee feh-veh-RAY-roo.
back: Fishermen offer flowers and perfume to Yemanja on February second.
context: Describing the seaside offerings to the Afro-Brazilian Afro-descendant Goddess of the Sea.

front: A Congada reconta o coroamento do rei do Congo com cantos e tambores.
pronunciation: ah kohng-GAH-dah rree-KOHN-tah oh koh-roo-ah-MEHN-toh doh RAY doh KOONG-goo kohm KAHN-toohz ee tam-BOH-rez.
back: The Congada reenacts the crowning of the king of Congo with songs and drums.
context: Discussing the traditional dramatic dance representing African monarchies.

front: As Cavalhadas encenam as históricas batalhas entre mouros e cristãos.
pronunciation: ahz kah-vahl-YAH-dahz ehn-SEH-nahm ahz ees-TOH-ree-kahz bah-TAH-lyahz EHN-tree MOH-oo-roohz ee krees-TOHNGZ.
back: The Cavalhadas stage the historical battles between Moors and Christians.
context: Describing a dramatic, highly colorful equestrian folk festival common in Central Brazil.

front: A mula sem cabeça é uma lenda que assustava crianças no interior.
pronunciation: ah MOO-lah sehng kah-BEH-sah eh OO-mah LEHN-dah keh ah-soos-TAH-vah kree-AHN-sahz noo een-teh-RYOHR.
back: The headless mule is a legend that used to scare children in the countryside.
context: Explaining the classic fire-breathing phantom animal from rural folklore.

front: O Boitatá é uma cobra de fogo que protege os campos contra queimadas.
pronunciation: oh boy-tah-TAH eh OO-mah KOH-brah jee FOH-goo keh proh-TEH-zhee ohz KAHM-poohz KOHN-trah kay-MAH-dahz.
back: The Boitata is a fire serpent that protects the fields from forest fires.
context: Introducing an ancient indigenous-derived fire spirit of the plains.

front: Muitas histórias rurais envolvem aparições do lobisomem nas noites de lua cheia.
pronunciation: MWEE-tahz ees-TOH-ryahz rroo-RAHEEZ ehn-VOHL-vehm ah-pah-ree-SOHNGZ doh loh-bee-ZOH-mehm nahz NOY-teez jee LOO-ah SHAY-ah.
back: Many rural stories involve sightings of the werewolf on full moon nights.
context: Highlighting the widespread Brazilian rural belief in werewolf transformations.

front: Minha fantasia para o bloco de Carnaval já está quase pronta.
pronunciation: MEENG-yah fahn-tah-ZEE-ah PAH-rah oh BLOH-koo jee kahr-nah-VAHL ZHAH ees-TAH KWAH-zee PROHN-tah.
back: My costume for the street parade is almost ready.
context: Casual planning phrase ahead of Carnival. Females use 'pronta', males use 'pronto'.

front: As escolas de samba ensaiam o ano inteiro para brilhar na avenida.
pronunciation: ahz es-KOH-lahz jee SAM-bah eheng-SAHEE-ahm oh AH-noo een-TAY-roo PAH-rah bree-LYAHR nah ah-veh-NEE-dah.
back: Samba schools rehearse all year long to shine on the avenue.
context: Referencing the immense dedication and rehearsal schedule of parade associations.

front: A micareta é como um Carnaval fora de época que atrai turistas.
pronunciation: ah mee-kah-REH-tah eh KOH-moo oong kahr-nah-VAHL FOH-rah jee EH-poh-kah keh ah-TRAHEE too-REES-tahz.
back: A micareta is like an off-season Carnival that attracts tourists.
context: Explaining the massive music festivals styled after regional Carnivals throughout the year.

front: Você precisa comprar o abadá oficial para entrar no bloco do trio.
pronunciation: voh-SEH pree-SEE-zah kohm-PRAHR oh ah-bah-DAH oh-fee-see-AHL PAH-rah ehn-TRAHR noo BLOH-koo doh TREE-oo.
back: You need to buy the official customized t-shirt to join the music truck's parade group.
context: Explaining the 'abadá'—the customized tank-top ticket for gated Carnival sections.

front: O quentão é uma bebida quente feita com cachaça, gengibre e cravo.
pronunciation: oh keheng-TOHNG eh OO-mah beh-BEE-dah KEHN-tee FAY-tah kohm kah-SHAH-sah, zheheng-ZHEE-bree ee KRAH-voo.
back: Quentão is a hot drink made with cachaça, ginger, and cloves.
context: Describing the famous spiced, warm cocktail drunk during cold June nights.

front: Pendurar as bandeirinhas coloridas faz parte da decoração da Festa Junina.
pronunciation: pehn-doo-RAHR ahz bahn-day-REEN-yahz koh-loh-REE-dahz FAHZ PAHR-tee dah deh-koh-rah-ZOHNG dah FES-tah zhoo-NEE-nah.
back: Hanging colorful little flags is part of the June Festival decoration.
context: Describing the standard festive, triangular flags seen in public squares.

front: No interior, é comum fazer simpatias para Santo Antônio no mês de junho.
pronunciation: noo een-teh-RYOHR, eh koh-MOONG fah-ZEER seem-pah-TEE-ahz PAH-rah SAN-too ahn-TOH-nyoo noo MEHS jee ZHOONG-yoo.
back: In the countryside, it is common to make love rituals to Saint Anthony in June.
context: Explaining the playful folklore of asking the 'matchmaker saint' for a partner.

front: O carimbó é uma dança folclórica com saias rodadas típica do Norte.
pronunciation: oh kah-reem-BOH eh OO-mah DAN-sah fohl-KLOH-ree-kah kohm SAHEE-ahz rroh-DAH-dahz TEE-pee-kah doh NOHR-tee.
back: Carimbó is a folkloric dance with spinning skirts typical of the North.
context: Introducing Pará's traditional indigenous and African-fused courtship dance.

front: O jongo é considerado uma das principais origens do samba moderno.
pronunciation: oh ZHONG-goo eh kohn-see-deh-RAH-doh OO-mah dahz preen-see-PAH-eez oh-REE-zhehengz doh SAM-bah moh-DEHR-noo.
back: Jongo is considered one of the main origins of modern samba.
context: Highlighting the traditional drum-circle dance brought by enslaved Bantu people.

front: O coco de roda é uma dança ritmada pela batida de pés e palmas.
pronunciation: oh KOH-koo jee ROH-dah eh OO-mah DAN-sah rreet-MAH-dah PEH-lah bah-TEE-dah jee PEHZ ee PAHL-mahz.
back: Coco de roda is a rhythmic dance driven by foot stamping and hand clapping.
context: Explaining a joyful coastal folk dance from northeastern Brazil.

front: O maculelê é uma dança folclórica encenada com bastões de madeira.
pronunciation: oh mah-koo-leh-LEH eh OO-mah DAN-sah fohl-KLOH-ree-kah ehn-seh-NAH-dah kohm bahs-TOHNGZ jee mah-DAY-rah.
back: Maculelê is a folkloric dance performed with wooden sticks.
context: Explaining a dramatic dance traditionally associated with Capoeira history.

front: A lenda do Negrinho do Pastoreio ajuda a encontrar objetos perdidos.
pronunciation: ah LEHN-dah doh neh-GREE-nyoh doh pahs-toh-RAY-oo ah-ZHOO-dah ah ehn-kohn-TRAHR oh-bjeh-toohz pehr-DEE-doohz.
back: The legend of Negrinho do Pastoreio helps people find lost items.
context: Introducing an Afro-Christian gaucho folklore myth from southern Brazil.

front: A lenda da Vitória-régia explica a origem de uma planta aquática gigante.
pronunciation: ah LEHN-dah dah vee-TOH-ryah REH-zhah ees-PLEE-kah ah oh-REE-zheheng jee OO-mah PLAN-tah ah-KWAH-tee-kah zhee-GAN-tee.
back: The legend of Victoria-regia explains the origin of a giant water plant.
context: Describing the indigenous Tupi-Guarani myth of a girl who fell into the river after trying to touch the moon.

front: O Caboclo de Lança é a figura mais emblemática do Maracatu Rural.
pronunciation: oh kah-BOH-kloo jee LAN-sah eh ah fee-GOO-rah MAHEES ehm-bleh-MAH-tee-kah doh mah-rah-kah-TOO rroo-RAHL.
back: The Lance Caboclo is the most iconic figure of Rural Maracatu.
context: Explaining Pernambuco's historical warrior figure clad in giant beads with a spear in his mouth.

front: Grupos de reisado cantam hinos religiosos e histórias nas praças públicas.
pronunciation: GROO-poohz jee rray-ZAH-doh KAHN-tahm EE-noohz rreh-lee-ZHEE-oh-zoohz ee ees-TOH-ryahz nahz PRAH-sahz POO-blee-kahz.
back: Reisado groups sing religious hymns and stories in public squares.
context: Describing theatrical caroling dynamic traditions common in the Northeast during the holidays.

front: A Festa de São Benedito celebra o padroeiro negro com muita Congada.
pronunciation: ah FES-tah jee SOHNG beh-neh-DEE-too seh-LEH-brah oh pah-droh-AY-roo NAY-groo kohm MWEE-tah kohng-GAH-dah.
back: The Feast of Saint Benedict celebrates the Black patron saint with a lot of Congada.
context: Highlighting a major religious-cultural event reflecting Afro-Brazilian pride.

front: O cordão de pássaros é uma manifestação folclórica teatral da Amazônia.
pronunciation: oh kohr-DOHNG jee PAH-sah-roohz eh OO-mah mah-nee-fes-tah-ZOHNG fohl-KLOH-ree-kah teh-ah-TRAHL dah ah-mah-ZOH-nee-ah.
back: The bird cordel is a theatrical folkloric manifestation of the Amazon.
context: Describing theatrical, musical plays centering around symbolic woodland birds.

front: A dança do pau de fita é muito popular no sul e sudeste do país.
pronunciation: ah DAN-sah doh PAHEE jee FEE-tah eh MWEE-too poh-poo-LAHR noo SOOL ee soo-DEHS-tee doh pah-EEZ.
back: The ribbon pole dance is very popular in the south and southeast of the country.
context: Describing a ribbon-weaving folk dance of European origin, performed around a central pole.

front: Essas manifestações folclóricas são reconhecidas como Patrimônio Cultural.
pronunciation: EHS-sahz mah-nee-fes-tah-SOHNGZ fohl-KLOH-ree-kahz SOHNG rreh-koh-nye-SEE-dahz KOH-moo pah-tree-MOHNG-yoo kool-too-RAHL.
back: These folkloric manifestations are recognized as Cultural Heritage.
context: Describing the official preservation status given to these unique traditions.
